decoded.explanation: The defendant is a first-time offender who committed a non-violent financial crime and has demonstrated remorse and an intent to make restitution by paying back a portion of the stolen funds. While embezzlement is a serious breach of trust, a 10-year sentence suggested by the spectator is disproportionately high for a $25,000 sum. Balancing the severity of the crime against the defendant's lack of a criminal record, a 6-month period of incarceration is appropriate to serve as both a deterrent and a recognition of the breach of fiduciary duty.
